'''Gesaril''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], found in the [[Gesaril system/Legends|Gesaril system]] of the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]]. It was the site of a series of accidents concerning Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|warships]].

The planet was covered with a dense jungle that appeared to float above a noxious swamp. It was inhabited by a [[Species/Legends|species]] of furry, hyperactive creatures called the [[Gesaril (species)|Gesaril]] that Imperial researchers had classified as barely [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]. There wasn't much of interest on the planet other than the wrecks of the nine Imperial [[Starship/Legends|spaceships]]. These ships all crashed in exactly the same coastal section of the planet, and the reason for this was not known. The planet had been put under strict quarantine, and no one was allowed to enter or leave.<ref name="GG6" />

==The sector prison==
The Empire had established a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] colony on a large [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in far [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Star/Legends|sun]]. Cells for 1,300 prisoners were hollowed out of this enormous rock, spaced widely along kilometers of corridor. A large tube in the center of the asteroid led to the ships' docks in the center of the rock. The [[Intrepid (Munifex-class)|''Intrepid'']] was stationed there to serve a dual purpose: to enforce the quarantine on Gesaril, and to guard the prison. The penal asteroid held the long-term prisoners of the Cluster. Holding at one point nearly 1,000 inmates, the prison population was about evenly divided between hardened criminals and political prisoners.<ref name="GG6" />
